Brief.RawText:
* @brief Arguments object that defines a Core event type, and provides a set of parameters specific to a given
 * event type.

Lines.FullText: Notably, core events trigger only on components that are reachable from the root of the openDAQ tree. Actions such as

Elements.RawText: Notably, core events trigger only on components that are reachable from the root of the openDAQ tree. Actions such as

Lines.FullText: adding properties during the creation of a function block will not trigger the event - only attaching the function block

Elements.RawText: adding properties during the creation of a function block will not trigger the event - only attaching the function block

Lines.FullText: to the tree will. Subsequent modification of the function block's properties, however, will trigger events, as the

Elements.RawText: to the tree will. Subsequent modification of the function block's properties, however, will trigger events, as the

Lines.FullText: function block is then reachable from the root.

Elements.RawText: function block is then reachable from the root.

Tags.RawText:
*
 * Notably, core events trigger only on components that are reachable from the root of the openDAQ tree. Actions such as
 * adding properties during the creation of a function block will not trigger the event - only attaching the function block
 * to the tree will. Subsequent modification of the function block's properties, however, will trigger events, as the
 * function block is then reachable from the root.

Lines.FullText: The Core Event object can be obtained from the Context object created by the Instance that is available to any component

Elements.RawText: The Core Event object can be obtained from the Context object created by the Instance that is available to any component

Lines.FullText: within the openDAQ tree structure.

Elements.RawText: within the openDAQ tree structure.

Tags.RawText:
*
 * The Core Event object can be obtained from the Context object created by the Instance that is available to any component
 * within the openDAQ tree structure.

 * `onValueWriteEvent`, providing the value of the property after said events are finished processing.

Elements.RawText: Eg. the path to the "MyInt" property of child object-type property named "Child1" on a component would be "Child1". In the case of

Lines.FullText: opendaq_core_event_types_component_added_removed Component added/removed

Elements.RawText: opendaq_core_event_types_component_added_removed Component added/removed

Tags.RawText: @subsubsection opendaq_core_event_types_component_added_removed Component added/removed

Lines.FullText: The Component added/removed events are triggered whenever a new component is attached or detached from

Elements.RawText: The Component added/removed events are triggered whenever a new component is attached or detached from

Lines.FullText: the openDAQ component tree. The event is only triggered when the added/removed component can be

Elements.RawText: the openDAQ component tree. The event is only triggered when the added/removed component can be

Lines.FullText: reached from the root of the tree. Eg. when creating a new function block, no event will be triggered

Elements.RawText: reached from the root of the tree. Eg. when creating a new function block, no event will be triggered

Lines.FullText: except for when the entire function block subtree is attached to the main tree.

Elements.RawText: except for when the entire function block subtree is attached to the main tree.

Tags.RawText:
*
 * The Component added/removed events are triggered whenever a new component is attached or detached from
 * the openDAQ component tree. The event is only triggered when the added/removed component can be
 * reached from the root of the tree. Eg. when creating a new function block, no event will be triggered
 * except for when the entire function block subtree is attached to the main tree.

Lines.FullText: The sender of the event is always the parent component of the added/removed child.

Elements.RawText: The sender of the event is always the parent component of the added/removed child.

Tags.RawText:
*
 * The sender of the event is always the parent component of the added/removed child.

*
 * Event triggered when an internal attribute of a component has been changed. Eg. when the "Active" state of the
 * component is modified.

Lines.FullText: The event has no preset parameters, but instead contains dictionary key-value pairs where the key corresponds to

Elements.RawText: The event has no preset parameters, but instead contains dictionary key-value pairs where the key corresponds to

Lines.FullText: The Attribute changed event payload always has a "AttributeName" key entry in the dictionary, containing the

Elements.RawText: The Attribute changed event payload always has a "AttributeName" key entry in the dictionary, containing the

Lines.FullText: name of the changed attribute.

Elements.RawText: name of the changed attribute.

Tags.RawText:
*
 * The Attribute changed event payload always has a "AttributeName" key entry in the dictionary, containing the
 * name of the changed attribute.

Lines.FullText: The payload then contains another entry using the "AttributeName" value as the key. That entry contains

Elements.RawText: The payload then contains another entry using the "AttributeName" value as the key. That entry contains

Lines.FullText: the new value of the attribute.

Elements.RawText: the new value of the attribute.

Tags.RawText:
*
 * The payload then contains another entry using the "AttributeName" value as the key. That entry contains
 * the new value of the attribute.

Lines.FullText: Currently the following attribute names can be present in the Attribute changed event payload:

Elements.RawText: Currently the following attribute names can be present in the Attribute changed event payload:

Tags.RawText:
*
 * Currently the following attribute names can be present in the Attribute changed event payload:

Lines.FullText: - Signal: "DomainSignal", "RelatedSignals", "Public"

Elements.RawText: - Signal: "DomainSignal", "RelatedSignals", "Public"

Lines.FullText: - Component: "Active", "Name", "Description", "Visible"

Elements.RawText: - Component: "Active", "Name", "Description", "Visible"

Tags.RawText:
*
 *  - Signal: "DomainSignal", "RelatedSignals", "Public"
 *  - Component: "Active", "Name", "Description", "Visible"

Lines.FullText: For the "Active" attribute, the payload contains additional entries depending on the cause of the change:

Elements.RawText: For the "Active" attribute, the payload contains additional entries depending on the cause of the change:

Lines.FullText: - "Active": the new effective active state of the component (always present)

Elements.RawText: - "Active": the new effective active state of the component (always present)

Lines.FullText: - "LocalActive": present when the component's own active state was changed directly by the user

Elements.RawText: - "LocalActive": present when the component's own active state was changed directly by the user

Lines.FullText: - "ParentActive": present when the active state changed due to a parent component's active state change

Elements.RawText: - "ParentActive": present when the active state changed due to a parent component's active state change

Tags.RawText:
*
 * For the "Active" attribute, the payload contains additional entries depending on the cause of the change:
 *  - "Active": the new effective active state of the component (always present)
 *  - "LocalActive": present when the component's own active state was changed directly by the user
 *  - "ParentActive": present when the active state changed due to a parent component's active state change

Lines.FullText: The native streaming server sends an RPC notification for the "Active" attribute change only when

Elements.RawText: The native streaming server sends an RPC notification for the "Active" attribute change only when

Lines.FullText: both "Active" and "LocalActive" are present in the payload (i.e. the change was triggered locally,

Elements.RawText: both "Active" and "LocalActive" are present in the payload (i.e. the change was triggered locally,

Lines.FullText: not propagated from a parent).

Elements.RawText: not propagated from a parent).

Lines.FullText: opendaq_core_event_types_device_connection_status Device connection status changed

Lines.FullText: Triggered whenever connection status of a device changes.

Elements.RawText: Triggered whenever connection status of a device changes.

Tags.RawText:
*
 * Triggered whenever connection status of a device changes.

Lines.FullText: The sender of the event is the device of which connection status changed.

Elements.RawText: The sender of the event is the device of which connection status changed.

Tags.RawText:
*
 * The sender of the event is the device of which connection status changed.

Lines.FullText: The event contains the following parameters:

Elements.RawText: The event contains the following parameters:

Lines.FullText: - The changed status name string under the key "StatusName"

Elements.RawText: - The changed status name string under the key "StatusName"

Lines.FullText: - The new status value encapsulated within an Enumeration object under the key "StatusValue". The possible values:

Elements.RawText: - The new status value encapsulated within an Enumeration object under the key "StatusValue". The possible values:

Lines.FullText: "Connected" - connection just established or restored, "Reconnecting" - connection lost, "Unrecoverable" - reconnection

Elements.RawText: "Connected" - connection just established or restored, "Reconnecting" - connection lost, "Unrecoverable" - reconnection

Lines.FullText: is not possible, "Removed" - connection removed (typically when corresponding streaming source is removed)

Elements.RawText: is not possible, "Removed" - connection removed (typically when corresponding streaming source is removed)

Lines.FullText: - The connection string associated with connection under the key "ConnectionString"

Elements.RawText: - The connection string associated with connection under the key "ConnectionString"

Lines.FullText: - The integer value specifying the type of particular connection which status changed (0 - Unknown, 1 - Configuration, 2 - Streaming,

Elements.RawText: - The integer value specifying the type of particular connection which status changed (0 - Unknown, 1 - Configuration, 2 - Streaming,

Lines.FullText: 3 - ConfigurationAndStreaming) under the key "ProtocolType"

Elements.RawText: 3 - ConfigurationAndStreaming) under the key "ProtocolType"

Lines.FullText: - The Streaming object associated with connection under the key "StreamingObject", the parameter is nullptr if connection

Elements.RawText: - The Streaming object associated with connection under the key "StreamingObject", the parameter is nullptr if connection

Lines.FullText: is of configuration type or the corresponding streaming connection has been removed

Elements.RawText: is of configuration type or the corresponding streaming connection has been removed

Lines.FullText: - The new status message under the key "Message", the parameter is nullptr if the corresponding connection has been removed

Elements.RawText: - The new status message under the key "Message", the parameter is nullptr if the corresponding connection has been removed

Tags.RawText:
*
 * The event contains the following parameters:
 *  - The changed status name string under the key "StatusName"
 *  - The new status value encapsulated within an Enumeration object under the key "StatusValue". The possible values:
 *  "Connected" - connection just established or restored, "Reconnecting" - connection lost, "Unrecoverable" - reconnection
 *  is not possible, "Removed" - connection removed (typically when corresponding streaming source is removed)
 *  - The connection string associated with connection under the key "ConnectionString"
 *  - The integer value specifying the type of particular connection which status changed (0 - Unknown, 1 - Configuration, 2 - Streaming,
 *  3 - ConfigurationAndStreaming) under the key "ProtocolType"
 *  - The Streaming object associated with connection under the key "StreamingObject", the parameter is nullptr if connection
 *  is of configuration type or the corresponding streaming connection has been removed
 *  - The new status message under the key "Message", the parameter is nullptr if the corresponding connection has been removed

Tags.RawText:
*
 * The ID of the event is 170, and the event name is "ConnectionStatusChanged".

Lines.FullText: opendaq_core_event_muting Muting core events

Elements.RawText: opendaq_core_event_muting Muting core events

Tags.RawText: @subsection opendaq_core_event_muting Muting core events

Lines.FullText: Components, as previously mentioned, do not trigger core events until they are connected to the root of the

Elements.RawText: Components, as previously mentioned, do not trigger core events until they are connected to the root of the

Lines.FullText: openDAQ tree. This is achieved through internal methods that enable/disable the triggers recursively. By default,

Elements.RawText: openDAQ tree. This is achieved through internal methods that enable/disable the triggers recursively. By default,

Lines.FullText: each component will not trigger any events - the triggers must be manually enabled. The SDK does this whenever

Elements.RawText: each component will not trigger any events - the triggers must be manually enabled. The SDK does this whenever

Lines.FullText: a component is added as a child of a parent, if the parent's core triggers are enabled. When done so, the triggers

Elements.RawText: a component is added as a child of a parent, if the parent's core triggers are enabled. When done so, the triggers

Lines.FullText: are enabled recursively for the entirety of the subtree. A converse function can be used to instead recursively

Elements.RawText: are enabled recursively for the entirety of the subtree. A converse function can be used to instead recursively

Lines.FullText: mute the events.

Elements.RawText: mute the events.

Tags.RawText:
*
 * Components, as previously mentioned, do not trigger core events until they are connected to the root of the
 * openDAQ tree. This is achieved through internal methods that enable/disable the triggers recursively. By default,
 * each component will not trigger any events - the triggers must be manually enabled. The SDK does this whenever
 * a component is added as a child of a parent, if the parent's core triggers are enabled. When done so, the triggers
 * are enabled recursively for the entirety of the subtree. A converse function can be used to instead recursively
 * mute the events.

Lines.FullText: Core events are triggered whenever a change in the openDAQ core structure happens. This includes changes to property values,

Elements.RawText: Core events are triggered whenever a change in the openDAQ core structure happens. This includes changes to property values,

Lines.FullText: addition/removal of child components, connecting signals to input ports and others. The event type can be identified

Elements.RawText: addition/removal of child components, connecting signals to input ports and others. The event type can be identified

Lines.FullText: via the event ID available within the CoreEventArgs object. Each event type has a set of predetermined parameters

Elements.RawText: via the event ID available within the CoreEventArgs object. Each event type has a set of predetermined parameters

Lines.FullText: available in the `parameters` field of the arguments. These can be used by any openDAQ server, or other listener to

Elements.RawText: available in the `parameters` field of the arguments. These can be used by any openDAQ server, or other listener to

Lines.FullText: react to changes within the core structure.

Elements.RawText: react to changes within the core structure.

Description.RawText:
*
 * Core events are triggered whenever a change in the openDAQ core structure happens. This includes changes to property values,
 * addition/removal of child components, connecting signals to input ports and others. The event type can be identified
 * via the event ID available within the CoreEventArgs object. Each event type has a set of predetermined parameters
 * available in the `parameters` field of the arguments. These can be used by any openDAQ server, or other listener to
 * react to changes within the core structure.
